name: Medi-Span (Wolters Kluwer) Drug Database API
what_it_does: Medi-Span (Wolters Kluwer Health) REST APIs for clinical drug database and pharmacy data platform. Enables AI agents to manage drug-drug interaction checking for clinical decision support, handle drug-allergy and drug-disease contraindication data retrieval for patient safety, access NDC (National Drug Code) and drug product data for pharmacy management, retrieve drug dosing guidelines and pediatric dosing recommendations for clinical applications, manage formulary and drug pricing data for pharmacy benefit management, handle medication therapy management (MTM) and polypharmacy review data, access generic equivalency and drug substitution data for pharmacy cost management, retrieve drug pregnancy and lactation safety data for prescribing support, manage drug supply shortage and discontinuation alert data, and integrate clinical drug data with EHR, pharmacy management, and clinical decision support systems.
use_cases: Checking drug interactions from clinical decision support agents
Validating prescriptions from pharmacy verification agents
Retrieving drug pricing from PBM formulary agents
Assessing medication safety from clinical pharmacology agents
not_for: Consumer drug information without clinical-grade interaction checking
Non-pharmacy applications without medication safety and clinical data needs
Simple drug lookups without comprehensive drug interaction database
best_when: A healthcare organization, pharmacy system, or clinical application using Medi-Span wants AI agents to automate drug interaction checking, prescription validation, formulary management, and clinical drug data integration.
avoid_when: PATIENT SAFETY RISK: Drug interaction and contraindication data is used for patient safety decisions — automated clinical drug checking without licensed pharmacist or prescriber review violates clinical safety standards. False negatives on drug-drug interactions can cause patient harm. Automated drug substitution and formulary overrides without clinical review creates prescribing errors. Clinical drug data has regulatory requirements for licensed healthcare use.

auth.notes: Medi-Span uses API key authentication. Per-organization credentials with licensed data access. Part of Wolters Kluwer Health. REST and flat-file database distribution options. No webhooks — licensed data updates via scheduled refresh. Indianapolis, Indiana HQ (Medi-Span). Clinical drug database licensed for specific healthcare use cases.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier — annual data license
pricing.paid_tiers: Annual license per use case (EHR, pharmacy, clinical decision support); redistribution requires separate licensing
pricing.notes: Indianapolis, Indiana. Medi-Span founded 1975. Part of Wolters Kluwer Health (acquired 1999). Clinical drug data market. 2,000+ healthcare organization customers. Drug interaction database licensed for EHR, pharmacy, and PBM integration. Competes with First Databank and Lexicomp for clinical drug database market.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per API key; healthcare application SLA requirements

known_agent_gotchas: PATIENT SAFETY RISK: Drug interaction false negatives are patient safety events; automated CDS using Medi-Span must not suppress or auto-override pharmacist or prescriber review
Licensed healthcare use only — Medi-Span data is licensed for specific healthcare use cases; consumer-facing or non-clinical use requires separate licensing review
Data update cadence — drug database is updated regularly (weekly/monthly); ensure automated systems consume data updates and don't use stale drug interaction data
Severity level interpretation — drug interaction severity ratings require clinical pharmacist interpretation; automated severity-based decisions without pharmacist oversight is inappropriate
NDC format complexity — NDC codes have multiple formats (10-digit, 11-digit, formatted with dashes); standardize NDC format before drug lookups to avoid missed matches

Clinical liability — automated clinical drug decisions based on Medi-Span data require licensed healthcare professional oversight; data licensor may require confirmation of clinical oversight in agreement
agent_readiness.security_notes: Clinical drug database. HIPAA, SOC2, 21CFR11. API key. US. Drug interaction and patient safety data.

security_score_components.security_notes: Clinical drug data. HIPAA, SOC2. API key. US. Drug interaction and medication data.
